page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g8ise.48xlarge is an x86_64 architecture server instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 192 dedicated physical cores and 192 vCPUs, utilizing Intel Xeon 8475B processors operating at a clock speed of 2.7 GHz. The instance is configured with 1 thread per core, providing dedicated compute resources without hyperthreading. It is equipped with 1024.0 GB of system memory, resulting in a memory-to-core ratio of 5.33 GB per core.

This instance does not include local NVMe or SSD storage, meaning all storage requirements must be met via network-attached block storage. For network connectivity, the instance provides a baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps. It does not feature any integrated GPU hardware or complimentary public IPv4 addresses. The lack of local storage and GPU acceleration represents a tradeoff, positioning this instance primarily for workloads that rely on high-capacity network storage and pure CPU execution.

Given its high vCPU count and balanced memory allocation, the ecs.g8ise.48xlarge is suited for large-scale general-purpose workloads, distributed enterprise applications, and CPU-intensive processing tasks that benefit from dedicated physical cores. The 100 Gbps network baseline supports high-throughput data transfers to external storage systems. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the high density of dedicated Intel Xeon cores and substantial memory capacity offers a consolidated platform for virtualization, container orchestration, and high-concurrency web serving.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g8ise.48xlarge is an x86_64 server instance utilizing the KVM hypervisor and powered by Intel Xeon 8475B processors running at 2.7 GHz. It delivers 192 dedicated vCPUs across 192 physical cores with a single thread per core configuration, paired with 1024.0 GB of system memory. The instance features a baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps but does not include local storage or GPU accelerators. This hardware profile makes it highly suitable for large-scale general-purpose applications, container orchestration, and high-throughput network-dependent workloads. The dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent performance for enterprise deployments requiring substantial compute density without hyperthreading overhead.

tagline: A high-density dedicated core instance featuring substantial memory and high-speed networking for demanding enterprise compute workloads.

1024.0 GB of system memory providing 5.33 GB per core.
100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for high-throughput data transfer.
